scriptRules.stages_1_4: hiragana and katakana focus, minimal kanji

items.notes: Used during the day. Not used in the morning or evening.
Use this at work or with strangers. 'おはよう' alone is casual.
Has a sense of finality. Japanese people often use じゃあね or またね for casual goodbyes.

items.target: ありがとうございます

stages.label: Beginner

items.notes: Incredibly important in Japan — open 24h, sell everything
Point at menu — works everywhere

items.target: アレルギーがあります

items.notes: CRITICAL: last trains in Japan are early — know this word!
Rechargeable transport card — easier than buying tickets
Very common konbini question — say はい or いいえ

items.target: ポイントカードはお持ちですか
items.romanji: pointo kaado wa omochi desu ka
items.meaning: Do you have a points card?
items.notes: Say いいえ (no) if you don't have one

items.target: チェックアウトは何時ですか
タオルをもっとください

items.notes: Important in ryokan/Japanese inns
Soft complaint — prices are generally fixed in Japan
Tax-free shopping available for tourists — very useful!
Most versatile word in Japanese — learn this first

items.target: ありがとうございます
よろしくお願いします
items.meaning: Nice to meet you / I'm counting on you
items.notes: Extremely important phrase in Japan — said in almost every new interaction

items.target: ちょっと待ってください
救急車を呼んでください
警察を呼んでください
地図を見せてもらえますか
items.meaning: Can you show me on a map?

items.target: タトゥーはありますか
items.notes: Many onsen ban tattoos — always check policies

items.target: かけ湯をしてください
もう一度お願いします
ゆっくり話してください

stages.description: A1: First words, katakana complete, basic phrases for Japan travel.

stages.label: Explorer

items.target: おいしくなかったです

items.notes: Written as は but read as wa when a particle
Polite: 食べます — use plain form with close friends
Core Japanese social experience — casual dining with drinks
Classic izakaya opener — everyone says this
Very common in Japan — splitting is normal

items.target: 今日 / 明日 / 昨日

sessions.title: あげる / もらう / くれる

items.notes: Most overused youth word — can mean amazing OR terrible depending on tone
Use this to show you're listening — very important in Japanese conversation
Said all the time when a staff member approaches or you've been served
Japanese indirect way of saying no — very important cultural cue

stages.description: A2-B1: Connected sentences, particles, casual conversation for daily life.

stages.label: Speaker

items.notes: Keigo for います — used by staff constantly
いただきます before eating — very important

items.target: 分からなければ聞いてください
items.meaning: If you don't understand, please ask
I want to live in Japan
I intend to / I plan to

items.notes: Said to everyone when leaving work — essential phrase
Always say this when leaving before others

items.target: 報告・連絡・相談 (ホウレンソウ)
items.notes: HoRenSo — the foundation of Japanese workplace communication
Major spring event — everyone has picnics under sakura trees

items.meaning: First shrine visit of the new year
It was eaten / I had it eaten

items.notes: Passive is often used for negative things that happened to you

items.target: それはいい考えですね
items.romanji: sore wa ii kangae desu ne

items.notes: Critical back-channel word — shows you're following along

items.target: ちょっと難しいですね
items.notes: Japanese avoid direct refusals — this IS a no

items.meaning: I'll think about it (often = soft no)

items.target: 忙しいから行けません
items.meaning: Because I'm busy, I can't go

items.notes: ので is more polite/softer than から
Kawaii culture is a genuine cultural phenomenon in Japan

items.target: 写真を撮ってもいいですか
items.romanji: shashin wo totte mo ii desu ka
items.notes: ALWAYS ask before photographing people or inside restaurants
映え (bae) = looks great on social media — a huge cultural trend

stages.description: B1: Real conversations, keigo intro, nuanced daily life.

stages.label: Scholar

items.notes: Relative clauses come BEFORE the noun in Japanese

items.meaning: Materials to use in the meeting

items.target: お世話になっております
items.meaning: Thank you for your continued support (email opener)
items.notes: Standard Japanese business email opening — always use this

items.target: 確認させていただきます
ご連絡お待ちしております
items.meaning: I look forward to hearing from you

sessions.title: ~てもらう/~てあげる/~てくれる

items.target: 手伝ってもらえますか
items.meaning: Could you help me? (requesting a favor)
I'll teach you (doing a favor for them)

items.target: 助けてくれてありがとう
富士山に登ったことがあります
items.romanji: fujisan ni nobotta koto ga arimasu

items.target: 刺身を食べたことがありません
items.romanji: sashimi wo tabeta koto ga arimasen

items.notes: The rainy season is a major feature of Japanese life

items.meaning: for the purpose of / in order to
so that I become good at it

items.target: もっと勉強すればよかった
行かなければよかった
ちょっと違うんですが…
items.meaning: This is a bit different from what I ordered...
items.notes: Japanese complaints are extremely indirect — this is considered assertive

items.target: 対応していただけますか
items.meaning: Could you handle this for me? (polite complaint)

items.target: 行かなければなりません
items.meaning: I must go / have to go
Someone with a fulfilling real life (internet slang)
lol / haha (internet: 草 = 笑 = laughing)
items.notes: www = laughing → looks like grass → 草 = lol

stages.description: B2: Complex grammar, advanced keigo, professional settings.

stages.label: Specialist

items.target: ご指摘ありがとうございます
items.meaning: Thank you for pointing that out

items.target: ご迷惑をおかけして申し訳ございません
items.romanji: gomeiwaku wo okake shite moushiwake gozaimasen
items.meaning: I sincerely apologize for the inconvenience

items.target: 早く寝たほうがいいよ
items.romanji: hayaku neta hou ga ii yo

items.target: 無理しないほうがいい

items.meaning: The way of the samurai / bushido code

items.notes: Meiji Era concept — Japan's approach to modernization

items.meaning: Fall seven times, get up eight
The nail that sticks out gets hammered down
items.notes: Reflects Japanese culture of conformity and not standing out

items.meaning: Even monkeys fall from trees (everyone makes mistakes)

items.target: 彼は来ると言っていました
items.romanji: kare wa kuru to itte imashita

items.target: 先生が宿題があると言いました
items.romanji: sensei ga shukudai ga aru to iimashita
items.meaning: The teacher said there is homework

items.notes: Uniquely Japanese concept of poignant sadness

items.meaning: What a waste / too good to throw away
items.notes: Deep Japanese value about not wasting — now international concept
Untranslatable Japanese word for a specific beautiful natural light

items.meaning: To read the atmosphere / social cues
items.notes: KY (空気読めない) = person who can't read the room — a social insult in Japan
Core Japanese cultural concept — what you feel vs what you say publicly

items.meaning: Holding back / restraint out of consideration

items.notes: Silence and space are meaningful in Japan — don't rush to fill them

items.meaning: Once in a lifetime encounter — treasure it

items.notes: Osaka people are famous for humor and this expression is iconic

stages.description: C1: Near-fluency for daily life, work, and social immersion in Japan.

stages.label: Archivist

items.notes: Found in old texts and formal poetry

items.meaning: indeed / it is so (classical emphasis particle)
Buying books and not reading them

items.notes: Serious social issue in Japan — the word exists because the phenomenon is common enough to need a name

items.meaning: Cold wind that signals winter's arrival
A, B, and others like that... (incomplete list)
To have a wide face = well connected
Scales fall from eyes = eye-opening revelation
To open one's stomach = speak frankly
One stone two birds = kill two birds with one stone
Or rather / I mean (casual fast filler)
What are you going to do? (very casual fast)

items.notes: These distinctions are softening in modern Japan but still recognized
Japanese is SOV — verb always comes last
The feeling that lingers after reading or experiencing something beautiful

sessions.title: Everything you need to live in Japan

items.target: すみません、コンビニはどこですか？
items.romanji: sumimasen, konbini wa doko desu ka?
items.meaning: Excuse me, where is the convenience store?

items.target: お会計をお願いします
ありがとうございました！またお越しください
items.meaning: Thank you very much! Please come again
items.notes: You'll hear this EVERY time you leave a store in Japan

stages.description: C2 target (functional fluency near-ceiling): Literature, dialect mastery, untranslatable concepts.

exchanges.text: いらっしゃいませ。何名様ですか？
exchanges.translation: Welcome. How many in your party?

exchanges.prompt: Tell them you are two people.

phraseLibrary.grammarNote: ～はどこですか is used to ask location of something.
Multi-purpose — use to get attention or apologize lightly.
